speechOptions.instruction: Speak in a bright, warm, hopeful tone — like happily introducing something you love to a friend. Gentle, positive energy throughout; never dark or flat.

title: Scattered Data, Connected
description: Introducing the new data-connecting features of MulmoClaude collections, built live on screen: backlinks and rollups added to a client list, invoices, and a work log — all by asking — until the AI answers questions that span collections.

beats.text: Hello! Today I'd like to show you a new set of features that connect the data in your MulmoClaude collections. Open a client's page, and their invoices and unbilled work hours are right there waiting. A view like this can now be built just by asking.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
New features
Your data is now connected
A client's page with their invoices and unbilled hours — all added by asking
💬 One page, with the related data gathered around it

beats.text: The new features are backlinks and rollups. Data you've been managing separately becomes one meaningful whole. Today, let's look at them one by one — by actually building with them.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
<div class='flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full text-center px-16'>
NEW IN MULMOCLAUDE COLLECTIONS
Backlinks · Rollups · A map of your data

beats.text: A collection is a dedicated home for data you want to manage as a table — like a client list or invoices. You can create one just by talking. How to build one is covered in another video.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
Basics
A dedicated home for table-style data — created just by talking
🎬 How to build one: see the "Collection Creation Demo" video

beats.text: As an example, let's take three collections: a client list, invoices, and a work log. Each invoice carries a link that says which client it's for. But until now there was no way to go the other direction — no seeing the invoices from the client's side.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
Basics
Links only point one way, invoice → client. No way back
<div class='grid items-center justify-center mt-10' style='grid-template-columns:auto auto auto;column-gap:36px;row-gap:32px'>

<div class='rounded-2xl px-10 py-7 text-center' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='0:0.28' data-start='2.21' data-end='2.71' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(67,20,7,.08);grid-column:1;grid-row:1'>
To: Sakura Design→

<div class='rounded-2xl px-12 py-14 text-center' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='0:0.12' data-start='1.14' data-end='1.64' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(67,20,7,.08);border:2px solid #EA580C;grid-column:3;grid-row:1 / span 2'>

Sakura Design
Tanaka Trading
Midori Publishing

<div class='rounded-2xl px-10 py-7 text-center' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='0:0.42' data-start='3.15' data-end='3.65' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(67,20,7,.08);grid-column:1;grid-row:2'>
Client: Sakura Design→
<div class='flex items-center justify-center gap-4 mt-9' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-sync='2:0.1' data-start='12.31' data-end='12.81'>
No way back — from Sakura Design's page, you couldn't see their invoices

beats.text: And building it? You just ask — like this. The AI reads the request and writes the setting for you. Nothing is written into your data files themselves.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 1
Ask for a backlink
One sentence, and the AI writes the setting for you
💬 "On each client's detail page, I'd like to also see the list of that client's invoices"

beats.text: Open the client's page, and there they are — that client's invoices, listed automatically. This is a backlink. The link that only pointed from invoice to client can now be followed in reverse.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 1
The backlink is in
The referenced side (client) now sees what references it (invoices)
↑ This client's invoices, lined up automatically

beats.text: Next, one more sentence: also show the total of the hours we haven't billed yet. And a new column appeared in the table. This is a rollup — when the underlying work hours change, the total updates automatically.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 2
Ask for a rollup
One more sentence, and a self-updating total appears
💬 "Also show the total of the work hours we haven't billed yet"

beats.text: Once your data is connected, the AI receives a map of the whole thing — a structure called an ontology. So questions that span collections, like which clients have unpaid invoices, get answered on the spot.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
The payoff
Connected data becomes the AI's map
Questions that span collections, answered on the spot

beats.text: Backlinks make the connections visible. Rollups keep the totals always in view. And the AI understands your data as a whole. All of it, just by asking. Give it a try!

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
<div class='flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full px-24'>

<div class='flex items-center gap-6 rounded-2xl px-8 py-4' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='0:0.0' data-start='0.29' data-end='0.79' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 6px 16px rgba(67,20,7,.06)'>

<div class='flex items-center gap-6 rounded-2xl px-8 py-4' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='1:0.0' data-start='2.29' data-end='2.79' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 6px 16px rgba(67,20,7,.06)'>

<div class='flex items-center gap-6 rounded-2xl px-8 py-4' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='2:0.0' data-start='4.76' data-end='5.26' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 6px 16px rgba(67,20,7,.06)'>
🗺️The AI sees the whole
A map of your data
All of it, just by asking.
🌱 Collections keep growing after they're built — give it a try!
